sites_used: invest.mp.gov.in (WordPress content pages + sws.invest.mp.gov.in React single-window shell)

policies.name: MP Industrial Promotion Policy 2025 + MP Investment Promotion Scheme 2025
policies.incentive_type: Capital-linked cash assistance (BIPA) + multipliers + sector add-ons + customized mega packages
policies.what_companies_get: Basic Investment Promotion Assistance (BIPA) of 40% down to 10% of Eligible Fixed Capital Investment on a published sliding formula (40% of EFCI up to Rs 50 Cr, tapering; hard cap Rs 200 Cr), paid in 7 equal annual instalments. Yearly BIPA is scaled by multipliers: Gross Supply Multiple (production >=40% of installed capacity yr-1; 75% of peak thereafter), Export Multiple 1.0-1.3 (ramps between 25% and 75% direct exports; fixed 1.0 in SEZs), Employment Multiple. EFCI add-ins: in-house R&D capped at 40% of P&M (pharma/biotech 50% incl. building), captive renewable power up to 50% of cost capped at 20% of P&M (needs >=51% self-consumption), energy-saving devices up to 20%. Separate heads: Green Industrialisation Assistance, Infrastructure Development Assistance, IPR/organic/quality-certification assistance, capital subsidy on testing facility (pharma/biotech/medical devices), cost of international technology transfer, assistance for units bringing FDI >26%. CCIP (CM-chaired cabinet committee) can sanction customized packages beyond policy for Mega units only.
policies.status: In force — policy PDF (Feb 2025) plus implementing Scheme notification (Jul 2025) both on portal
policies.application_status: Open — incentive application templates (new/expansion/review/appeal), SOP, checklist and offline calculators for IPP-2014 and IPP-2025 live on /ccip-and-incentives/; sanction orders published through Jul 2025
policies.site_notes: Root invest.mp.gov.in redirects to sws.invest.mp.gov.in (JS shell), but the WordPress pages /policy-acts-rules/ and /ccip-and-incentives/ serve full content to curl. BIPA formula and multiplier details extracted from the IPP 2025 PDF itself (pdftotext). Historic CCIP sanction orders (Trident, UltraTech, P&G, Pepsico, BPCL Bina etc.) published on-site — real disbursal trail.

policies.name: Chhattisgarh Industrial Development Policy 2024-30
policies.what_companies_get: Key features stated on the official portal: 100% stamp duty exemption; 50% capital investment subsidy; 50% interest subsidy; 50% ETP (effluent treatment) subsidy; 50% technical patent subsidy; 50% technology purchase subsidy; 20% employment assistance; 75% EPF reimbursement. Full policy PDF downloadable; an interactive Subsidy Calculator (invest.cg.gov.in/calculator) lets a company compute its own entitlement.
policies.application_status: Open — investor registration and applications via 'One Click Single Window System' (swsportal.cgstate.gov.in), Express Investment Intent form live
policies.site_notes: Percentages are the portal's own headline figures; caps/tenures and the core-sector (steel/cement) vs sunrise-sector differentiation are inside the policy PDF, not extracted. industries.cg.gov.in unreachable (connection timeout) — invest.cg.gov.in is the working official face.

policies.name: Chhattisgarh Logistics Policy 2025
policies.what_companies_get: Not extracted — policy PDF verified present on portal

policies.name: Industrial Policy Resolution (IPR) 2022 (+ two amendments)
policies.incentive_type: Umbrella policy — capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement, power/electricity-duty, land, employment
policies.what_companies_get: Capital investment subsidy 20% (Priority) / 30% (Thrust sector) of P&M with NO upper limit, disbursed over 5 years; 100% net SGST reimbursement capped at 200% of P&M cost (300% in Biju Economic Corridor); 100% electricity-duty exemption 7y (Priority) / 10y (Thrust); power tariff reimbursement Rs 2/unit for 7y/10y; 100% ESI+EPF employer-contribution reimbursement 5y/7y for state-domiciled workers; 100% stamp duty exemption on IDCO/govt land; land at concessional rates (50% of concessional rate for Thrust units employing >1000); green infra subsidy 25% up to Rs 10 Cr (ZLD equipment 50% up to Rs 10 Cr); private industrial parks 50% of infra cost up to Rs 25 Cr; R&D 50% up to Rs 10 Cr; captive RE plants 30% capital subsidy + 20-year ED/transmission exemptions.
policies.status: In force — remains the operative industrial policy on the portal (no IPR 2025 successor listed as of 2026-07-20)
policies.application_status: Open — operational guidelines for each incentive head published; applications via GO-SWIFT single window (linked under EoDB)
policies.site_notes: Full incentive table read from the page; ~20 operational-guideline documents listed, indicating live administration. 'HLC Special Package of Incentives' notification also present.

policies.name: Green Hydrogen / Green Ammonia incentives (embedded in IPR 2022)
policies.incentive_type: Sectoral incentives inside umbrella policy — NOT a standalone GH2 policy
policies.what_companies_get: GH2/green-ammonia manufacturing units: 100% electricity-duty exemption for 20 years; power tariff reimbursement Rs 3/unit (from DISCOMs/GRIDCO) for 20 years; 20-year exemption/reimbursement of cross-subsidy surcharge, additional surcharge and state transmission charges on renewable energy; GH2/ammonia is a Thrust Sector (so 30% capital subsidy, SGST reimbursement etc. also apply).
policies.application_status: Open (dedicated operational guideline for the surcharge/transmission reimbursement published)
policies.site_notes: No separate 'Green Hydrogen Policy' document exists on the portal — the brief's standalone-policy framing is wrong; incentives live inside IPR 2022 and the Renewable Energy Policy 2022.

policies.name: Food Processing Policy 2022 (covers food & seafood processing)
policies.what_companies_get: Not extracted from the policy page; note that 'Food and Sea-food Processing' is an IPR-2022 Priority Sector, so those units also get the 20%-capital-subsidy/SGST/power stack above
policies.status: In force (listed among current sectoral policies)
policies.site_notes: Policy listed in the live nav; detail page not fetched.

policies.name: MSME incentive schemes (Silpasathi 'Incentives/Subsidies/Schemes')
policies.what_companies_get: MSMEs entitled to: State Capital Investment Subsidy; interest subsidy on term loan; power subsidy; energy-efficiency subsidy; stamp duty & registration fee subsidy; SGST subsidy; water-conservation/environment-compliance subsidy; standard-quality-compliance subsidy; workforce-welfare assistance; patent registration subsidy. Percentages/caps are in the linked scheme notification PDFs (byte-served, not fetched). Site states explicitly: 'The State has no credit schemes at present for business. Only grants are provided to Business through the above two schemes' (MSME + Tourism).
policies.status: In force — scheme notifications + online application links live
policies.application_status: Open — 'Apply Online' buttons per scheme on Silpasathi
policies.site_notes: Silpasathi portal is fully live (revamped; QRT helpline; licenses/NOCs/renewals operational). Tourism incentive scheme similarly listed (capital subsidy, interest subsidy, electricity-duty waiver, employment-generation incentive, mega-project subsidy).

policies.name: New West Bengal Incentive Scheme (large industries, reportedly announced 2025)
policies.what_companies_get: COULD NOT VERIFY on official state web properties. Silpasathi's notifications page (through Jul-2025 entries) carries only RTPS-timeline and process notices — no WBIS-2025 notification; the incentives page covers only MSME + Tourism and states only those two grant schemes exist.
policies.status: Unverified — no trace on reachable official portals
policies.site_notes: WBIDC.com — the natural home for a large-industry scheme — is suspended (see access issues), so absence of evidence here is weak evidence of absence. Treat media claims of a 2025 WBIS as unconfirmed by official sources. Textile incentive scheme and data-centre policy: also not found on Silpasathi.

policies.name: Jharkhand Industrial and Investment Promotion Policy (JIIPP) 2021
policies.what_companies_get: Percentages not published on the HTML page (in the policy PDF). The portal operationalizes it end-to-end: 'Know your Incentives' eligibility checker (/incentive/incentives/check), 'Apply for Incentives' module, SOP of Incentive, Incentive Guideline, and a Common Incentive Disbursement Guideline — i.e., incentive workflow is live, not just a document.
policies.status: In force — listed under current downloadable policies (JIIPP 2016 relegated to the archive list; NO JIIPP 2025 successor on site as of 2026-07-20)
policies.application_status: Open — online incentive application + Combined Application Form (CAF) live on the Single Window
policies.site_notes: Active policy list also includes: Jharkhand Electric Vehicle Policy (no year in link title), Ethanol Production Promotion Policy 2022, Export Policy 2023, Food & Feed Processing Industry Policy 2024, MSME Promotion Policy 2023, Industrial Park and Logistic Policy, State Solar Policy 2022, Tourism Policy 2021, and the Jharkhand MSME (Special Concession) Act 2025.

policies.what_companies_get: Not extracted — listed with download on the official policies page (title carries no year)
policies.site_notes: Brief's 'textile policy': only Textile, Apparel and Footwear Policy 2016 exists, and it sits in the ARCHIVED list — no current textile policy on site.

policies.name: Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana (MMUY) + Bihar Laghu Udyami Yojana (BLUY)
policies.incentive_type: Entrepreneur financing (subsidy + interest-free loan) — micro/small, not large-company incentives
policies.what_companies_get: Amounts not stated on the fetched pages (guidelines PDF 'MMUY 2024-25 Selection Process & Guidelines' hosted on portal). What IS verified: the scheme is actively administered — FY 2025-26 beneficiary selection done by computerized lottery on 20-05-2026, document-upload deadline 24-06-2026, utilization-certificate deadline 30-06-2026 for FY22-25 cohorts receiving 1st/2nd instalments.
policies.status: In force, actively running FY 2025-26 cycle
policies.application_status: FY 2025-26 application window CLOSED (extended to 23-03-2026); currently in post-selection document/disbursement phase
policies.site_notes: This is the only live incentive-delivery portal found for Bihar's Dept of Industries.

policies.name: BIADA Land Allotment & Management Policy 2026
policies.incentive_type: Industrial land/shed allotment (plug-and-play sheds, 9 regional clusters)
policies.what_companies_get: Industrial land and plug-and-play shed allotment terms under the new 2026 policy (PDF + Jun-2026 corrigendum on site); Amnesty Policy portal for defaulting allottees also live
policies.application_status: Open — online land/shed application with user manual on BIADA portal
policies.site_notes: BIADA acts/policies page carries only BIADA's own instruments — no state industrial policy.

site_access_issues: wbidc.com: account SUSPENDED — serves cPanel suspendedpage.cgi embedding an iframe to a third-party ad/redirect domain (iyfhshsp.com). The flagship WBIDC domain is effectively hijacked-by-parking; do not trust any content there.
industries.cg.gov.in: connection timeout (invest.cg.gov.in works and is the operative portal).
udyog.bihar.gov.in: DNS does not resolve (NXDOMAIN) — the historical Dept of Industries portal is gone.
swc.bihar.gov.in, invest.bihar.gov.in, industries.bihar.gov.in: DNS does not resolve.
biharbusinessconnect.com: 302-redirects to ww547.biharbusinessconnect.com (classic parked/lapsed-domain pattern) — the summit domain appears to have lapsed; not followed for safety.

invest.mp.gov.in root: redirects to sws.invest.mp.gov.in React JS shell (empty to curl); content only reachable via direct WordPress paths (/policy-acts-rules/, /ccip-and-incentives/). /cms/wp-json returns the WP 404 theme page (wp-json REST not exposed at that path).
startup.bihar.gov.in: live but pure JS shell (Vite bundle) — no content without a browser.
state.bihar.gov.in/industries: reachable but is an IPRD template with no departmental policy content.
silpasathi.wb.gov.in scheme notifications are served as base64-parameterized byte streams (read-bytea-file-all/...) — PDF contents (percentages/caps) not extractable within fetch budget.

CG: Chhattisgarh Innovation and Startup Promotion Policy 2025-2030, CG Logistics Policy 2025, CSR facilitation portal, RAMP, Export Promotion section, online Subsidy Calculator

Odisha: Semicon Fabless Policy 2023, Biotechnology Policy 2024, Pharmaceutical & Medical Devices Policy 2025, PSP Policy 2025, Data Centre Policy 2022, IT Policy 2022, Renewable Energy Policy 2022, Apparel & Technical Textiles Policy 2022, Logistics Policy 2022, MSME Development Policy 2022, Long Term Linkage Policy of Minerals 2023, HLC Special Package of Incentives notification, captive-RE-plant incentive block and private-industrial-park capital grant inside IPR 2022

Bihar: Bihar Laghu Udyami Yojana (BLUY) alongside MMUY, BIADA Land Allotment & Management Policy 2026 + Amnesty Policy
